name: Pizza-Style Stuffed Chicken
summary: Chicken stuffed with pizza sauce, pepperoni, and melted mozzarella. Sounds like a gimmick. Tastes like a good idea.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 205°C (400°F).
Butterfly the chicken breasts — cut through the middle without going all the way through.
Stuff each breast with pizza sauce, pepperoni, green pepper, and mozzarella. Secure closed with toothpicks.
Place on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Season with pepper. Bake 18–20 minutes until no longer pink inside.

name: Piri Piri Chicken
summary: A whole spatchcocked chicken, slowly basted on the BBQ with a garlicky piri piri sauce. Patience required. Worth it.

steps.instruction: In a pot, melt the butter. Add oil, garlic, bacon, piri piri sauce, oregano, and paprika. Cook over medium heat to develop the sauce.
Spatchcock the chicken: cut along both sides of the backbone and remove it, then flatten the bird. Pierce all over with a knife. Season generously with salt.
Preheat the BBQ. Place chicken skin-side down over indirect heat for 20 minutes.
Flip skin-side up. Spoon sauce slowly over the chicken. Cook 20 minutes. Flip and cook 20 more minutes. Flip one final time and cook another 20 minutes, basting throughout.

name: Chicken Samosas
summary: Shredded chicken and cheese folded into tortilla triangles, baked until crispy. A lunchbox legend.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 375°F. Cut each tortilla in half.
On each half, place 2 tbsp shredded chicken and 1 tbsp cheese near the rounded edge. Fold the rounded edge over to just cover the filling.
Fold the pointed tip over to form a triangle. Keep folding the triangle over itself until you reach the end. Tuck the remaining tip inside to seal.
Brush samosas with oil and bake on a lined sheet for 12 minutes until golden.
Mix sour cream, yogurt, chili seasoning, and lime juice for the dipping sauce. Serve hot or cold.

name: Pork Souvlaki
summary: Marinated pork tenderloin on skewers, grilled hot and fast. Lemon wedge non-negotiable.

steps.instruction: Cut pork tenderloin into 1-inch cubes.
Combine vinegar, olive oil, garlic, mustard, oregano, paprika, salt, and pepper to make the marinade. Toss pork in marinade and refrigerate at least 2 hours.
Remove from fridge 30 minutes before cooking. Thread pork and chunks of red onion onto skewers.
Grill on a hot BBQ for 4 minutes per side. Rest covered for 5 minutes. Serve with a lemon wedge.

name: French Onion Soup
summary: Onions, slowly caramelised, finished with miso and beer. Melted cheese on top. No notes.

steps.instruction: In a large pot over medium heat, cook the onions in oil for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until soft and golden.
Deglaze with beer or wine and let reduce for 3 minutes.
Add basil, oregano, bay leaves, miso, and vegetable broth. Stir well. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 30 minutes. Season if needed.
Preheat oven broiler. Place oven-safe bowls on a baking sheet. Ladle soup in, add a baguette crouton to each, and cover generously with cheese. Broil 5 minutes until bubbling and golden.

name: Lentil Soup
summary: Red lentils, warm spices, canned tomatoes. On the table in 45 minutes. Serve with naan and zaatar.

steps.instruction: Soften the onion in olive oil in a large pot. Season with salt and pepper.
Add turmeric, cumin, and coriander. Stir for 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
Add broth, lentils, and tomatoes. Season again. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer gently for 20 minutes until lentils are tender. Add more broth if needed.
Stir in fresh cilantro. Adjust seasoning. Serve with naan and zaatar.

name: Antipasto Salad
summary: Grilled halloumi, hearts of palm, olives, and a punchy red wine vinaigrette. Looks like effort. Takes 20 minutes.

steps.instruction: Slice and prepare all salad vegetables.
Grill halloumi in a dry pan with dried oregano until golden on both sides.
Whisk together red wine vinegar, oil, Dijon, sugar, salt, and pepper.
Arrange everything on a platter or toss in a bowl. Drizzle with dressing and serve immediately.

name: Love Salad
summary: A Ricardo rice salad with ginger-soy dressing, cashews, spinach, and raisins. The kind of salad people ask for the recipe of.

steps.instruction: In a small saucepan, cook the garlic and ginger in 2 tbsp of the oil until golden. Transfer to a bowl, discard the ginger. Add remaining oil, soy sauce, and honey. Mix well.
In a large bowl, combine all salad ingredients.
Pour dressing over the salad. Season with pepper. Toss and serve immediately.

name: Italian Pasta Salad
summary: Penne, salami, cherry tomatoes, and a maple-dijon vinaigrette. The lunchbox pasta salad that holds up by Thursday.

steps.instruction: Cook pasta in salted boiling water until al dente. Drain, rinse with cold water, drain again.
Whisk together oil, red wine vinegar, maple syrup, Dijon, and Italian seasoning in a large bowl.
Add pasta, lettuce, salami, tomatoes, and onion to the bowl. Toss well to combine.

name: Quinoa and Lentil Salad
summary: Quinoa, lentils, feta, cucumber, kale — the salad that actually keeps you full. Apple cider vinaigrette.

steps.instruction: Cook quinoa according to package instructions. Spread out to cool.
Dice all vegetables and combine in a large bowl with drained lentils, quinoa, feta, and parsley.
Whisk together olive oil, apple cider vinegar, lemon juice, honey, Dijon, salt, and pepper.
Toss salad with dressing. Adjust seasoning and serve.

name: Beet and Carrot Bread
summary: A dense, moist loaf with grated beets and carrots folded through. Ends up a deep purple. Tastes better than it sounds.

steps.instruction: In a large bowl, mix oil, sugar, egg yolks, hot water, and vanilla. Add grated beets and carrots. Mix well.
In a separate bowl, sift together flour, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon. Fold dry ingredients into the wet mixture. Add walnuts if using.
Beat egg whites to stiff peaks. Gently fold into the batter — don't deflate them.
Pour into a greased baking pan. Bake at 350°F for 25 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.

name: Choco-Mocha Energy Balls
summary: Dates, oats, cocoa, and a hit of coffee blended into 30 fudgy little balls. They keep in the fridge all week. Allegedly.

steps.instruction: Add all ingredients to a food processor. Blend until a uniform mixture forms and holds together when pressed.
Roll into 30 balls using about 1 tbsp of mixture each.
Store in the refrigerator. Freezes well.

name: Date Squares
summary: Mom's recipe. A thick layer of sticky date filling between two oat crumble layers. The ones from the bakery aren't as good.

steps.instruction: Date filling: cook dates and water over medium heat until thick. Remove from heat and stir in orange and lemon juices.
Oat crumble: mix all remaining ingredients together until crumbly.
Press half the oat mixture firmly into a greased 9×13" pan. Spread date filling evenly over it. Top with the remaining oat crumble.
Bake at 325°F for 30–35 minutes until golden. Cool completely before cutting into squares.

name: Lemon Tart
summary: Mom's lemon curd, made in the microwave, poured into a shell and left to set. Sharper than you expect. That's the point.

steps.instruction: Mix sugar, cornstarch, salt, water, lemon zest, and lemon juice in a microwave-safe bowl.
Microwave on high for 5–7 minutes, whisking every 2 minutes, until thickened.
Temper the egg yolks: stir a spoonful of the hot mixture into the beaten yolks, then whisk the yolks back into the bowl.
Microwave on medium for 2 more minutes. Stir in margarine until melted. Pour into the pre-baked tart shell. Refrigerate until set.

name: Molasses Cookies
summary: Grandma's recipe. Spiced with ginger, cinnamon, and cloves. Rolled thin, cut into shapes. The house smells incredible.

steps.instruction: Cream butter and sugar together in a large bowl. Add molasses and egg. Mix well.
Combine all dry ingredients. Add to the butter mixture alternately with the milk.
Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and form a ball. Flatten into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Roll dough to ½ inch thickness and cut into shapes. Place 1½ inches apart on parchment-lined baking sheets. Bake 12–15 minutes.

name: Vanilla Frosting
summary: Mom's three-ingredient frosting. On a cake, on a cupcake, or eaten directly from the bowl. No judgement.

steps.instruction: Beat all ingredients together until smooth and spreadable. Add a touch more milk if needed to reach your desired consistency.

name: Quick Pickles
summary: Lebanese cucumbers in a honey-vinegar brine. Ready after a few hours in the fridge. Katerine's recipe.

steps.instruction: Mix water, vinegar, and honey until honey dissolves.
Add the sliced cucumbers. Refrigerate for a few hours before serving.

name: Fried Pork and Cilantro Meatballs
summary: Ground pork meatballs packed with fresh cilantro, pan-fried until golden. Simple, fragrant, satisfying.

steps.instruction: Combine pork, cilantro, garlic, soy sauce, sesame oil, egg, and breadcrumbs. Mix well. Form into golf ball-sized meatballs.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry meatballs in batches, turning occasionally, for 10–12 minutes until cooked through and golden on all sides.
Drain on paper towels. Serve with rice or noodles.

name: Burrito Bowl
summary: Spiced ground beef over rice with black beans, corn, and all the toppings. Weeknight dinner at full volume.

steps.instruction: Brown the ground beef and onion in a skillet over medium-high heat. Drain excess fat.
Season with chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Stir in salsa. Simmer 5 minutes.
Build bowls: rice, then beef mixture, then black beans and corn. Top with cheese, sour cream, avocado, cilantro, and a squeeze of lime.

name: Rice Krispie Squares
summary: Butter, marshmallows, Rice Krispies. You know this recipe. It's perfect. Don't change anything.

steps.instruction: Melt butter in a large pot over low heat. Add marshmallows and stir until completely melted. Remove from heat.
Add Rice Krispies and stir until evenly coated.
Press firmly into a buttered 9×13" pan. Let cool to room temperature before cutting into squares.

name: BBQ Pork Ribs
summary: Dry-rubbed ribs slow-cooked in the oven, then finished on the BBQ with a sticky homemade sauce. A weekend project with a clear reward.

steps.instruction: Remove the membrane from the back of the ribs. Mix brown sugar, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dry mustard, cayenne, and salt into a dry rub. Coat ribs generously on all sides.
Wrap ribs tightly in foil. Bake at 275°F for 2.5–3 hours until tender.
While ribs bake, combine ketchup, apple cider vinegar, Worcestershire, honey, and hot sauce in a small saucepan. Simmer 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Unwrap ribs and brush generously with sauce. Grill over medium heat for 10–15 minutes, turning and basting until caramelized.

name: Thin Crepes
summary: The classic batter — eggs, milk, flour, butter. Makes about 12 thin crepes. Sweet or savoury, your call.

steps.instruction: Whisk together eggs, milk, flour, melted butter, and salt until smooth. Let batter rest for 15 minutes.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Lightly butter the pan. Pour a small ladle of batter and swirl to coat the pan thinly.
Cook about 1 minute until edges start to lift and the surface is set. Flip and cook 30 seconds more. Stack and repeat.

name: Tuna Bread Cups
summary: Sliced bread pressed into muffin tins, filled with a creamy tuna mixture, and baked until crispy. A retro classic that still works.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 375°F. Flatten each bread slice with a rolling pin. Press into greased muffin tin cups to form little bread bowls.
Mix tuna, mayo, celery, onion, mustard, and lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper.
Fill each bread cup with tuna mixture. Top with shredded cheese.
Bake 15–18 minutes until edges are golden and cheese is melted.

name: Deep-Dish Pizza
summary: Thick, focaccia-style crust topped with chunky tomato sauce, sausage, and a lot of cheese. Chicago by way of the family kitchen.

steps.instruction: Oil a 9×13" baking pan generously. Press dough into the pan and up the sides to form a thick crust. Let rest 15 minutes.
Brown the sausage in a skillet, breaking it apart. Drain. Add garlic, crushed tomatoes, oregano, basil, and red pepper flakes. Simmer 10 minutes.
Spread mozzarella over the dough. Top with sausage sauce. Sprinkle Parmesan over everything.
Bake at 425°F for 25–30 minutes until crust is golden and cheese is bubbling.

name: Pineapple Dessert
summary: A no-bake pineapple cream dessert layered with graham crackers. Needs to be made the day before. Patience rewarded.

steps.instruction: Beat cream cheese and powdered sugar together until smooth. Fold in drained pineapple and whipped topping.
Layer graham crackers in the bottom of a 9×13" pan. Spread half the pineapple cream over top.
Add another layer of graham crackers. Spread remaining cream over top. Finish with a final layer of crackers.
Cover and refrigerate overnight before serving.

name: Al Pastor Pork
summary: Pork shoulder marinated in chili, achiote, and pineapple, then roasted or grilled. Tacos optional but strongly encouraged.

steps.instruction: Blend soaked chilies, achiote, pineapple juice, vinegar, garlic, oregano, and cumin until smooth.
Toss pork slices in the marinade. Refrigerate at least 2 hours, or overnight.
Grill or roast pork at 400°F for 20–25 minutes until charred at the edges. Add pineapple chunks in the last 5 minutes.
Chop meat and serve in warm tortillas with diced onion, cilantro, and pineapple.

name: Lemon Chicken
summary: Chicken thighs braised in a bright lemon-herb sauce. Straightforward enough for Tuesday, good enough for company.

steps.instruction: Season chicken with salt and pepper. Sear in olive oil over medium-high heat, skin-side down, for 5 minutes until golden. Flip and cook 3 more minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add garlic and cook 1 minute. Add lemon juice, zest, broth, thyme, rosemary, and honey. Stir.
Return chicken to the pan, skin-side up. Simmer over medium-low heat, covered, for 20–25 minutes until cooked through. Spoon sauce over before serving.

name: Candied Orange Peels
summary: Orange peels blanched three times, simmered in syrup, then dried and rolled in sugar. An old-school confection that takes all day but keeps for weeks.

steps.instruction: Score orange peels into quarters and remove. Cut into strips. Place in a pot, cover with cold water. Bring to a boil. Drain. Repeat this process 3 times to remove bitterness.

Combine sugar and water in the pot. Bring to a boil, stirring until sugar dissolves. Add blanched peels and simmer over low heat for 45–60 minutes until peels are translucent and syrup is mostly absorbed.
Remove peels with tongs and lay on a wire rack to dry for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Roll dried peels in sugar. Store in an airtight container.

name: Oatmeal Chocolate Chip Cookies
summary: Classic oatmeal cookies with dark chocolate chips. Crisp edges, chewy centre. No raisins. You're welcome.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 350°F. Beat butter and both sugars together until fluffy. Add eggs and vanilla. Mix well.
Mix in flour, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt. Fold in oats and chocolate chips.
Drop rounded tablespoons onto parchment-lined baking sheets. Bake 10–12 minutes until edges are golden. Let cool on the pan for 5 minutes before transferring.

name: Coconut Macaroons
summary: Three ingredients, one bowl. Chewy, dense coconut mounds with crispy golden edges. Better dipped in chocolate.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 325°F. Mix coconut, condensed milk, and vanilla in a bowl.
Beat egg whites and salt to stiff peaks. Fold gently into the coconut mixture.
Drop heaping tablespoons onto parchment-lined baking sheets. Bake 20–25 minutes until golden brown. Cool completely before removing.

name: Pad Thai
summary: Rice noodles, shrimp, eggs, and bean sprouts in a tamarind-fish sauce. Real pad thai flavour, made at home.

steps.instruction: Soak rice noodles in warm water for 30 minutes. Drain. Mix tamarind, fish sauce, and sugar in a small bowl to make the sauce.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat. Stir-fry shrimp until pink. Push to the side, scramble the eggs in the centre.
Add noodles and garlic. Pour sauce over. Toss everything together for 2–3 minutes until noodles absorb the sauce.
Add bean sprouts and green onions. Toss briefly. Serve topped with crushed peanuts and a lime wedge.

name: Banana Bread
summary: The three-banana classic. Moist, not too sweet, gets better on day two. Add chocolate chips if you need to be convinced.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 350°F. Mash bananas in a large bowl. Stir in melted butter.
Mix in sugar, egg, and vanilla. Sprinkle baking soda and salt over the mixture and stir. Fold in flour until just combined — don't overmix.
Pour into a buttered 4×8" loaf pan. Bake 55–60 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean. Cool in the pan for 15 minutes before turning out.

name: Pizza Dough
summary: A reliable yeast dough that works for thin crust, deep dish, or anywhere in between. Makes enough for two pies.

steps.instruction: Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm water. Let sit 5–10 minutes until foamy.
Stir in olive oil and salt. Add flour one cup at a time, mixing until a shaggy dough forms. Turn onto a floured surface and knead for 8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place in an oiled bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm spot for 1 hour until doubled.
Punch down the dough. Divide into two portions. Use immediately or wrap and refrigerate for up to 2 days.

name: Pâté Chinois
summary: Quebec's answer to shepherd's pie. Ground beef and creamed corn under a layer of mashed potatoes. Golden on top. Comfort in a pan.

steps.instruction: Boil potatoes until tender. Drain and mash with butter, warm milk, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Brown ground beef and onion in a skillet. Season with salt and pepper. Drain excess fat.
Spread beef mixture in a greased 9×13" baking dish. Layer creamed corn and whole kernel corn over the beef. Spread mashed potatoes evenly on top.
Bake at 375°F for 20–25 minutes until the potato top is lightly golden. Broil the last 2 minutes for extra colour.

name: Pesto Cauliflower Pasta
summary: Roasted cauliflower tossed with pasta and basil pesto. One of those meatless pastas that doesn't feel like a compromise.

steps.instruction: Toss cauliflower florets with olive oil, garlic, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet. Roast at 425°F for 20–25 minutes until golden.
Cook pasta in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve ½ cup pasta water before draining.
Toss hot pasta with pesto, roasted cauliflower, a splash of pasta water, and lemon juice.
Plate and top with Parmesan and toasted pine nuts.

name: Marry Me Chicken Pasta
summary: Creamy sun-dried tomato sauce with chicken over pasta. Named that for a reason.

steps.instruction: Season chicken with salt, pepper, and dried herbs. Sear in olive oil over medium-high heat, 4 minutes per side until golden. Set aside.
In the same pan, cook garlic 1 minute. Add sun-dried tomatoes, broth, and cream. Simmer 5 minutes until slightly thickened.
Stir in Parmesan and red pepper flakes. Return chicken to the pan and simmer 5 more minutes.
Toss with cooked pasta, adding pasta water if needed. Top with fresh basil.

name: Laotian Grilled Chicken
summary: Chicken marinated in lemongrass, fish sauce, and galangal, then grilled over charcoal. The marinade does the work.

steps.instruction: Blend or mince lemongrass, galangal, and garlic together. Mix with fish sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, and sesame oil to make the marinade.
Score the chicken pieces deeply so the marinade penetrates. Coat thoroughly and marinate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight.
Grill over medium heat, turning frequently, for 35–40 minutes until cooked through with charred edges. Baste with leftover marinade as it cooks.
Rest 5 minutes. Serve with steamed rice and cilantro.

name: Poke Bowl
summary: Ahi tuna marinated in soy and sesame, served over rice with avocado, edamame, and pickled cucumber. Better than the one at the mall.

steps.instruction: Toss diced tuna with soy sauce, sesame oil, rice vinegar, and sriracha. Let marinate 10 minutes.
Divide warm rice between two bowls.
Arrange tuna, avocado, edamame, and cucumber over the rice. Top with green onions, sesame seeds, and a drizzle of remaining marinade.

name: Miso Ginger Chicken
summary: Chicken thighs glazed in white miso, ginger, and honey, roasted until caramelised. Goes with rice, noodles, or just as is.

steps.instruction: Whisk miso, ginger, garlic, honey, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil together. Reserve 2 tbsp of marinade for glazing. Coat chicken in the rest and marinate 30 minutes or overnight.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Arrange chicken in a single layer in a baking dish.
Roast 25–30 minutes, brushing with reserved glaze halfway through, until caramelised and cooked through. Top with sesame seeds and green onions.

name: Chicken Satay
summary: Lemongrass-marinated chicken skewers served with a rich peanut dipping sauce. The sauce is the point.

steps.instruction: Mix lemongrass, fish sauce, turmeric, and sugar. Toss chicken strips in the marinade and thread onto skewers. Marinate at least 30 minutes.
Whisk peanut butter, coconut milk, soy sauce, lime juice, garlic, and sriracha together for the dipping sauce. Warm gently if needed.
Grill skewers over medium-high heat for 3–4 minutes per side until cooked through and lightly charred. Serve with peanut sauce.

name: Chicken Rice-a-Roni
summary: Chicken thighs over broken spaghetti and rice, all cooked together in seasoned broth until golden and fluffy. A one-pot weeknight staple.

steps.instruction: Season chicken with paprika, salt, and pepper. Sear in butter and oil over medium-high heat, 4 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, toast broken spaghetti pieces and rice until golden, about 3 minutes. Add onion and garlic, cook 2 minutes.
Pour in chicken broth and thyme. Nestle chicken thighs on top. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer 20 minutes until rice is tender and chicken is cooked through.
Rest covered for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork, top with parsley, and serve.

name: Rocky Road
summary: Melted dark chocolate with marshmallows, nuts, and whatever else you want to throw in. Refrigerate and cut into squares.

steps.instruction: Melt chocolate chips, butter, and condensed milk together in a double boiler or microwave in 30-second intervals, stirring until smooth.
Remove from heat. Fold in marshmallows and nuts.
Spread into a parchment-lined 8×8" pan. Refrigerate at least 2 hours until firm. Cut into squares.

name: St-Hubert Style Coleslaw
summary: A creamy, lightly sweet coleslaw that tastes suspiciously close to the one from the chicken chain. Paired with anything off the grill.

steps.instruction: Combine shredded cabbage and grated carrot in a large bowl.
Whisk together mayo, vinegar, sugar, celery salt, and onion powder until smooth.
Pour dressing over the slaw and toss well. Refrigerate at least 30 minutes before serving for the flavours to meld.

name: Corn and Blueberry Salad
summary: Grilled corn, fresh blueberries, red onion, and feta in a honey-lime dressing. A summer salad that genuinely surprises people.

steps.instruction: Grill or roast corn. Cut kernels off the cob.
Combine corn, blueberries, red onion, feta, and cilantro in a bowl.
Whisk lime juice, honey, and olive oil together. Pour over salad. Toss and serve at room temperature or chilled.

name: Udon Miso Chicken Salad
summary: Chewy udon noodles with shredded chicken, red cabbage, and edamame in a white miso dressing. Cold, satisfying, and better the next day.

steps.instruction: Whisk miso, rice vinegar, soy sauce, sesame oil, and honey until smooth.
Combine cooled noodles, shredded chicken, cabbage, edamame, carrot, and green onions in a large bowl.
Pour dressing over and toss to coat. Top with sesame seeds. Serve cold.

name: Ham and Pasta Salad
summary: Rotini with diced ham, cheese cubes, and vegetables in a creamy dressing. The pasta salad that shows up at every gathering and disappears first.

steps.instruction: Cook pasta in salted water until al dente. Drain and rinse under cold water.
Whisk together mayo, sour cream, vinegar, dill, salt, and pepper.
Combine pasta with ham, cheese, vegetables, and dressing. Toss well. Refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving.

name: Laotian Larb
summary: Minced pork with toasted rice powder, fish sauce, lime, and loads of fresh herbs. One of those dishes that tastes completely alive.

steps.instruction: Toast uncooked rice in a dry pan over medium heat, shaking constantly, until golden and fragrant, about 5 minutes. Grind in a mortar or spice grinder to a coarse powder.
Cook ground pork in a dry pan over medium-high heat, breaking it apart, until just cooked through. Remove from heat.
Toss warm pork with fish sauce, lime juice, shallots, chili, and sugar. Let stand 2 minutes.
Add mint, cilantro, and green onions. Sprinkle toasted rice powder over top. Toss gently and serve immediately with sticky rice.

name: Vietnamese Salad
summary: Rice noodles, shredded chicken, cucumber, and herbs in a classic nuoc cham dressing. Fresh, bright, and ready in 20 minutes.

steps.instruction: Whisk fish sauce, lime juice, sugar, garlic, and chili together to make the dressing. Taste and adjust balance.
Combine noodles, chicken, cucumber, carrot, bean sprouts, mint, and cilantro in a large bowl.
Pour dressing over and toss well. Top with crushed peanuts and serve immediately.

name: Pineapple Hot Sauce
summary: A blended fresh hot sauce with scotch bonnets, pineapple, and ginger. Takes 15 minutes. Keeps for weeks. Use it on everything.

steps.instruction: Combine all ingredients in a blender. Blend until smooth.
Taste and adjust heat, acidity, and salt to your liking. Add more vinegar or lime for brightness.
Strain through a fine mesh sieve for a smoother sauce, or leave as-is for more texture. Pour into a sterilised jar. Refrigerate for up to 4 weeks.

name: Mediterranean Grilled Salmon
summary: Salmon fillets marinated in lemon, olive oil, garlic, and herbs, then grilled quickly. Dinner in under 30 minutes.

steps.instruction: Mix olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, thyme, paprika, and salt. Coat salmon fillets and marinate 15 minutes.
Grill over medium-high heat for 4 minutes per side, or until the fish flakes easily.
Serve topped with capers and fresh parsley.

name: Grilled Salmon with Peanut Ginger Sauce
summary: Salmon grilled with a soy glaze, served over a warm peanut-ginger sauce. An Asian-inspired riff that earns its place in the rotation.

steps.instruction: Mix soy sauce, honey, and sesame oil. Brush over salmon fillets.
Whisk together peanut butter, ginger, garlic, lime juice, coconut milk, and sriracha until smooth. Warm gently in a small saucepan.
Grill salmon over medium-high heat, 4 minutes per side until cooked through.
Spoon peanut ginger sauce over each fillet. Top with green onions and serve.
